parts.label: Primary Structure (3U)

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on the solar panel face due to extreme temperature cycling.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management system to handle temperature variations.

risk.desc: Risk of deployment shock damaging the burn-wire release mechanism.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough testing of the deployment shock before final assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of alignment errors during assembly leading to improper fit.
risk.mit: Ensure precise alignment tools are used during assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical connection failure due to vibration during launch.
risk.mit: Conduct vibration testing on electrical connections during launch simulation.

risk.desc: Risk of structural integrity issues due to launch vibration.
risk.mit: Conduct structural analysis and testing to ensure integrity.

physics_paths.path_description: Deployable solar panel wing experiences launch vibration up to 20g and solar pressure during deployment.
Solar panel absorbs sunlight and radiates heat, must maintain operational temperature between -40 to +80C.
Spring-loaded hinge allows for deployment and retraction of the solar panel wing.
Electrical connections from solar cells to the main power distribution board.
Burn-wire release mechanism for deployment in orbit.

integration_opportunities.opportunity_description: Ensure the hinge mechanism is properly integrated with the solar panel frame and deployment damper.
Ensure the electrical connections from the solar cells are routed to the main power distribution board.
Ensure the thermal management system is integrated to handle the temperature variations.

verification_focus.focus_description: Verify the mechanical strength of the hinge mechanism and separation spring plate.
Verify the electrical connections from the solar cells to the main power distribution board.
Verify the thermal performance of the solar panel under extreme temperature conditions.

Heat generated by motor and bearing dissipated to surrounding structure via conduction.
